info.name: yt-dlp flatPlaylist inspection
info.description: Documentation-only collection. Postman cannot execute yt-dlp flags directly because yt-dlp-exec is a local process wrapper, not an HTTP API. Use these requests as the exact flag presets to compare. The existing captured fixtures in test/channel.json, test/thebeatagp-flat.channel.json, and test/thebeatagp.channel.json are the reference payloads for inspection.

request.description:
Equivalent yt-dlp-exec call:

youtubedl("{{channelRoot}}", {
  dumpSingleJson: true,
  skipDownload: true,
  flatPlaylist: true,
  playlistItems: "0"
})

Use this when you want top-level channel/container metadata without enumerating actual videos.

Fixture source: test/channel.json

Equivalent yt-dlp-exec call:

youtubedl("{{channelRoot}}/videos", {
  dumpSingleJson: true,
  skipDownload: true,
  flatPlaylist: true,
  playlistStart: 1,
  playlistEnd: 3
})

This returns shallow video entries. In the existing fixture, the first actual video entry is a url-style object with keys like title, id, duration, _type, and url, but not full description/formats/tags.

Fixture source: test/thebeatagp-flat.channel.json

Equivalent yt-dlp-exec call:

youtubedl("{{channelRoot}}/videos", {
  dumpSingleJson: true,
  skipDownload: true,
  flatPlaylist: false,
  playlistStart: 1,
  playlistEnd: 3
})

This returns fully extracted nested video objects inside the playlist response. In the existing fixture, the first actual video entry already contains fields like formats, description, webpage_url, categories, tags, duration, view_count, and upload_date.

Fixture source: test/thebeatagp.channel.json
